statement.informal: `voltage_step` satisfies its @verify contract: under preconditions (voltage >= V_MIN ∧ voltage <= V_MAX ∧ m_gate >= 0.0 ∧ m_gate <= 1.0 ∧ h_gate >= 0.0 ∧ h_gate <= 1.0 ∧ n_gate >= 0.0 ∧ n_gate <= 1.0 ∧ g_na >= 0.0 ∧ g_na <= G_MAX ∧ g_k >= 0.0 ∧ g_k <= G_MAX ∧ g_l >= 0.0 ∧ g_l <= G_MAX ∧ abs(e_na) <= V_MAX ∧ abs(e_k) <= V_MAX ∧ abs(e_l) <= V_MAX ∧ abs(i_ext) <= I_MAX ∧ cm >= CM_MIN ∧ cm <= CM_MAX ∧ dt > 0.0 ∧ dt <= DT_MAX), the function's result satisfies (True). This is the obligation Forge ships to a Lean target for DO-178C / IEC-61508 / ISO-26262 certification evidence.
statement.formal_lean:
-- @verify obligation pulled from hodgkin_huxley_step.eml
-- theorem hh_voltage_bounded_under_step : ∀ args, (voltage >= V_MIN ∧ voltage <= V_MAX ∧ m_gate >= 0.0 ∧ m_gate <= 1.0 ∧ h_gate >= 0.0 ∧ h_gate <= 1.0 ∧ n_gate >= 0.0 ∧ n_gate <= 1.0 ∧ g_na >= 0.0 ∧ g_na <= G_MAX ∧ g_k >= 0.0 ∧ g_k <= G_MAX ∧ g_l >= 0.0 ∧ g_l <= G_MAX ∧ abs(e_na) <= V_MAX ∧ abs(e_k) <= V_MAX ∧ abs(e_l) <= V_MAX ∧ abs(i_ext) <= I_MAX ∧ cm >= CM_MIN ∧ cm <= CM_MAX ∧ dt > 0.0 ∧ dt <= DT_MAX) → (True) := sorry
